prediction.predicted_approach: Claim 1 says 12 Pydantic models with a regex parser. I predict: EvidenceClass as a StrEnum mapping A-F, ArtifactLink with a from_url classmethod that detects mutable branches via regex, Claim/IntentSection/VerificationPacket as frozen BaseModels, ValidationFinding with a rule_id field, and computed properties on VerificationPacket for evidence_classes_present. The parser will use re.compile patterns to split markdown by ## headers, extracting sections into a dict, then constructing a VerificationPacket. Claim 2 says 7-stage pipeline: I predict a ValidationPipeline class with a validate() method that calls each validator sequentially, collecting findings by severity. Claim 3 says Typer CLI: I predict app = typer.Typer() with check and init commands using Rich console for formatted output. Claim 4 says guard sanitization: I predict functions like sanitize_shell_input that strip dangerous characters. Claim 5 says diff analyzer: I predict a DiffAnalyzer class that parses unified diff format and detects critical surfaces via regex on filenames. Claim 6 says frozen models: I predict ConfigDict(frozen=True) on every model. Claim 7 says 36/36 tests: I predict the test suite covers model construction, validation edge cases, and integration flows.
expected_edge_cases: Parser fails on markdown with ## inside fenced code blocks
EvidenceClass.from_string receives an unknown class letter
AntiCheatScanner scan_diff receives diff with no test file changes

traces.trace_notes: Traced ArtifactLink.from_url for Claim 1. Step 1: urlparse(url) extracts netloc and path. Step 2: if github.com in netloc, splits path by /. Step 3: if actions+runs in path, returns immutable github_actions immediately. Step 4: if blob/tree in path, extracts the ref segment (path_parts[idx+1]). Step 5: checks if ref is a hex SHA >= min_sha_length chars. Step 6: checks if ref matches known mutable branches (main/master/develop/staging/trunk/dev/HEAD). Step 7: SHA returns immutable, mutable branch returns is_immutable=False, else returns unknown non-SHA reference. Step 8: GitHub PR links (pull in path) always immutable. Step 9: all other URLs return external+mutable. PREDICTION COMPARISON: I predicted from_url as a classmethod with mutable branch detection - CONFIRMED. I did NOT predict the configurable mutable_branches parameter or the min_sha_length parameter. OBSERVATION: A 7-char hex string like 'abcdef1' passes the SHA check but could be a branch name. The min_sha_length=7 default is reasonable but not foolproof.
traces.edge_case_tested: URL with /blob/abcdef1/ where abcdef1 is actually a branch name
traces.predicted_output: Returns is_immutable=True incorrectly because 7 hex chars passes the SHA check

traces.trace_notes: Traced PacketParser.parse for Claim 1. Step 1: HEADER_PATTERN.search checks for '# AIV Verification Packet', raises PacketParseError if missing. Step 2: _extract_sections splits by newline, iterates lines, matches HEADING_PATTERN (^#{1,6}\s+), builds ParsedSection list with level/title/content/line ranges. Step 3: _parse_intent finds section with 'Class E' in title, extracts URL links and requirements. Step 4: _parse_claims finds section with 'Claim' in title, matches NUMBERED_ITEM_PATTERN on each content line. Step 5: _enrich_claims_with_evidence attaches evidence_class/artifact to each claim from evidence subsections. Step 6: _collect_evidence_classes scans for all ### Class X subsections. Step 7: _parse_classification extracts risk_tier from yaml block. PREDICTION COMPARISON: I predicted regex-based section splitting - CONFIRMED. I did NOT predict the substance checking (_MIN_SUBSTANCE_ALPHA=10, _ALWAYS_PLACEHOLDER_RE) or the enrichment step. OBSERVATION: _extract_sections matches HEADING_PATTERN against every line including inside fenced code blocks. A code block containing '## Heading' would be misidentified as a section boundary. This confirms my edge case prediction.
traces.edge_case_tested: Markdown with ## inside a fenced code block (triple backtick)
traces.predicted_output: Parser incorrectly splits at the code block heading, creating a malformed section with partial code as content

traces.trace_notes: Traced the 7-stage pipeline for Claim 2. Stage 1 (Parse): parser.parse(body) catches PacketParseError, returns FAIL with E001 immediately — this is the only early-return stage. Stage 2 (Structure): StructureValidator checks intent section exists, claims present, claim descriptions >=10 chars, reproduction instructions, evidence class+artifact. Stage 3 (Links): LinkValidator calls ArtifactLink.from_url on each link, blocks on mutable branches (E004), warns on non-SHA refs (E009). Stage 4 (Evidence): EvidenceValidator dispatches to 6 class-specific validators, checks bug fixes have Class F (E010). Stage 5 (Risk-Tier): _check_tier_requirements uses _TIER_REQUIRED dict to enforce evidence classes per tier — R0 needs A+B, R1 needs A+B+E, R2 needs A+B+C+E, R3 needs all six. Missing required class blocks with E019. Stage 6 (Zero-Touch): ZeroTouchValidator scans for prohibited patterns like 'run locally' or 'execute the script', blocks with E008. Stage 7 (Anti-Cheat): if diff provided, AntiCheatScanner.scan_diff checks for deleted assertions, skip decorators, mock.patch additions. Stage 8 (Cross-Reference): unjustified anti-cheat findings block with E011. Final: strict_mode makes warnings blocking too. OBSERVATION: Claim 2 says 7-stage pipeline but code has 8 stages (anti-cheat cross-reference is separate). The docstring also says 7. This is a minor documentation drift, not a code bug.
traces.edge_case_tested: Packet with all required evidence classes but a mutable branch link in Class E
traces.predicted_output: Stage 3 (Links) produces E004 BLOCK finding, but Stages 4-8 still run and may produce additional findings. Final result: FAIL with E004 plus any other findings.

traces.trace_notes: Traced the check command for Claim 3. Step 1: Reads config from --config flag or creates default AIVConfig with strict_mode. Step 2: Body input handling has 4 paths: stdin (body=='-'), file path (Path(body).exists()), raw string, or missing (exit 1). Step 3: Reads diff from --diff file path if provided. Step 4: Creates ValidationPipeline(cfg) and calls pipeline.validate(body_text, diff_text). Step 5: Rich output - creates a Table with rule_id, severity, message, location columns. Iterates errors first (red), then warnings (yellow), then info (dim). Step 6: Exit code is 1 if result.status != PASS. PREDICTION COMPARISON: I predicted Typer + Rich - CONFIRMED. I predicted check and init commands - need to verify init exists separately. OBSERVATION: The check command reads stdin when body=='-' but does not handle the case where body is None and stdin has data piped in. A user doing 'cat packet.md | aiv check' without the '-' argument gets the 'No packet body provided' error.

state_transitions.after_value: (piped stdin content — but body arg is None, not '-')

traces.edge_case_tested: Piping stdin without the '-' argument: cat packet.md | aiv check
traces.predicted_output: Error: No packet body provided, exit code 1 - the stdin data is silently ignored

traces.trace_notes: Traced AntiCheatScanner.scan_diff for Claim 2 (anti-cheat portion). Step 1: Splits diff by newline, iterates lines. Step 2: On 'diff --git' line, extracts file path via regex r'b/(.+)$', increments files_analyzed, checks _is_test_file. Step 3: On @@ hunk header, extracts line number. Step 4: On deleted lines (starting with -), checks: assertion_patterns for deleted asserts (DELETED_ASSERTION), skip_patterns for added @skip decorators (SKIPPED_TEST), bypass_patterns for added mock.patch (BYPASS_FLAG). Step 5: On added lines (starting with +), checks skip and bypass patterns. Step 6: Separately checks for removed test files via 'deleted file mode' in diff. Step 7: Returns AntiCheatResult with all findings, files_analyzed, test_files_modified counts, and requires_justification computed property. OBSERVATION: The scanner only looks at test files (matched by test_file_patterns). If someone deletes an assertion in a non-test file (e.g. a validation check in production code), it is NOT flagged. This is by design per the spec but is a potential blind spot. Also, the regex r'b/(.+)$' for file path extraction could match incorrectly if the filename itself contains 'b/' — unlikely but possible with unusual directory names.

state_transitions.after_value: [] (deleted assertion on '-assert user.is_admin' line skipped — not a test file)

traces.edge_case_tested: Diff where production code assertion 'assert user.is_admin' is deleted from src/auth.py (not a test file)
traces.predicted_output: No finding produced because src/auth.py does not match test_file_patterns - the deletion is invisible to anti-cheat

findings.description: Parser _MIN_SUBSTANCE_ALPHA=10 is an undocumented magic number threshold for minimum alphabetic characters in substance check
E020 rule_id collision between pipeline.py (optional evidence tier info) and evidence.py (Class A code blob warning) - two different rules share the same ID

why_questions.question: Why does the pipeline not short-circuit on Stage 2-6 errors the way it does on Stage 1 parse errors?
why_questions.context: Stage 1 returns FAIL immediately on PacketParseError but all other stages accumulate errors and continue running

falsification_scenarios.scenario: AUDIT CORRECTED: Original said 12 models, actual is 13 (missed FrictionScore). Also 4 of 13 are Enums not BaseModels. Corrected test: import all 13 named classes from aiv.lib.models and verify the 9 BaseModel subclasses have frozen=True
Pass a packet with a /blob/main/ link through ValidationPipeline.validate and verify E004 BLOCK finding is produced
Run 'aiv check' on a valid packet file and verify exit code 0 with Rich-formatted table output on stdout

AUDIT CORRECTED: Original referenced hallucinated sanitize_shell_input function which does not exist. Claim 4 mentions guard sanitization but no such function exists in the codebase. Scenario cannot be executed as written — the prediction was wrong.

AUDIT CORRECTED: Original referenced hallucinated DiffAnalyzer class which does not exist. Actual implementation is AntiCheatScanner.scan_diff. Corrected test: call AntiCheatScanner.scan_diff with a diff containing auth.py changes and verify findings are produced for test files only.
Check that every model class in models.py has ConfigDict(frozen=True) - if any model has frozen=False, claim 6 is falsified

AUDIT CORRECTED: Original said exactly 36 tests but current suite has 371. The claim was about the original commit. Corrected test: run pytest tests/unit/test_models.py tests/unit/test_parser.py tests/unit/test_pipeline.py --collect-only and verify the original test files exist with > 0 tests.

probe.overall_assessment: FINDINGS: (1) Parser does not handle ## headings inside fenced code blocks - _extract_sections matches HEADING_PATTERN on every line unconditionally. A verification packet containing a code block with '## Example' would be mis-parsed. (2) E020 rule_id collision: pipeline.py line 229 uses E020 for optional evidence info, evidence.py also uses E020 for Class A code blob warnings. Two different rules share an ID. (3) Pipeline docstring claims 7 stages but code implements 8 (cross-reference is separate from anti-cheat scan). (4) AntiCheatScanner only checks test files - production code assertion deletions are invisible. (5) CLI stdin requires explicit '-' argument - piping without it silently fails. No AI tells detected in model definitions or validator logic. The dispatch pattern in EvidenceValidator._validate_claim_evidence is clean and extensible.
